description_long: This is a vintage-style biplane with a bright yellow fuselage and deep blue wings, featuring a classic double-wing structure with struts and braces. The propeller is mounted at the nose and is made of wood, with a visible engine cowling behind it. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted yellow with blue accents. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, all fixed and exposed. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft, with a robust and functional appearance.
description: A vintage yellow and blue biplane with double wings, wooden propeller, and open cockpit, featuring fixed landing gear and classic tail design.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the yellow tail, blue double wings, and the side of the fuselage.
description_view_1: Direct rear view highlighting the tail structure, twin horizontal stabilizers, and the full span of the double wings.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the wooden propeller, engine cowling, and the stacked blue wings with visible struts.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the wooden propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical double wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, staggered wings mounted high and low on the fuselage, connected by vertical struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a small fixed tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
